source_pr.title: planner: support using `with <opt>=<val>` to set options when running index advisor
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: ref #12303

Problem Summary: planner: support using `with <opt>=<val>` to set options when running index advisor

### What changed and how does it work?

planner: support using `with <opt>=<val>` to set options when running index advisor.
For example: `recommend index with max_num_index=5`

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: The test collected recommended index names from query results and compared them as a joined string. The row order (and therefore the order of collected index names) is not guaranteed to be stable, so the test could intermittently fail when the same set of indexes was returned in a different order. The fix sorts the `indexes` slice (`sort.Strings`) before comparison, making the assertion deterministic.
fix_pattern: When asserting on an unordered set of returned rows/items, sort the collected values (e.g., index names) before comparing expected output to avoid nondeterministic ordering flakiness.

evidence: files: pkg/executor/builder.go, pkg/executor/executor.go, pkg/parser/ast/misc.go, pkg/parser/misc.go, pkg/parser/parser.go, pkg/parser/parser.y, pkg/parser/parser_test.go, pkg/planner/core/common_plans.go, pkg/planner/core/planbuilder.go, pkg/planner/indexadvisor/BUILD.bazel, pkg/planner/indexadvisor/indexadvisor.go, pkg/planner/indexadvisor/indexadvisor_test.go, pkg/planner/indexadvisor/indexadvisor_tpch_test.go, pkg/planner/indexadvisor/options.go, pkg/planner/indexadvisor/options_test.go
